_meta.method: Web research across Kwan Um Europe directory, Sanbo Zen International, Plum Village Israel community site, Jerusalem Zen Center site, IRIZ/Hanazono Zen centers list, and Wikipedia. Cross-checked teacher and lineage attribution.
_meta.coverageEstimate: ~85% of publicly visible active Zen-lineage practice places in Israel. The Plum Village 'Community of Mindfulness in Israel' lists ~12 local sanghas under one umbrella organisation; we record the umbrella with a note rather than each weekly meeting group, since most do not publish standalone addresses or websites.
knownGaps: Individual local Plum Village sanghas (Ein Hod, Hadera, Haifa, Jerusalem x3, Mevasseret, Netanya, Rehovot, Tel Aviv x2) are aggregated under mindfulness-israel.org and not separately enumerated here.
No verified active One Drop Zen, Daihonzan Chozenji, or Vietnamese Thien (non-Plum-Village) sitting group found in Israel.
Hidden Zen Center / hiddenzen.org / hsbis.org could not be verified — domains did not resolve and no third-party directory lists them; treated as unverified and excluded.

Hasharon, Ramat Gan, and Pardes Hanna Kwan Um groups are referenced in Wikipedia and the Kwan Um Europe network but lack independent live websites; included only when an authoritative directory entry exists.

places.name: Tel Aviv Zen Group (Kwan Um)
places.lineage: Kwan Um School of Zen (Korean Seon, Seung Sahn lineage)
places.address: 24A HaRav Reines St, Tel Aviv, Israel
places.notes: Founded January 1999 by Revital Dan, who trained directly with Zen Master Seung Sahn in Korea. The principal Kwan Um center in Israel; serves as hub for the smaller Israeli Kwan Um groups.

places.name: Jerusalem Zen Center
places.notes: Founded 2003; co-founder and teacher Daniel Stambler trained in both Soto and Rinzai traditions in Asia and the West. Lay-led; weekly Sunday evening zazen.

places.name: Sanbo-Zen Israel
places.notes: Israeli affiliate of Sanbo Zen International. Teaching link is to Migaku Sato Roshi (Kyuun-ken), whose countries of activity include Israel; local contact Anders Englund Weiss. Activity coordinated via Facebook page.

places.name: Zen in Daily Life (Soto, Danny Waxman)
places.notes: Layman Soto-Zen teacher Danny Waxman, indoor disciple of Prof. Masunaga Reiho; trained in Japan 1958-1971. Zazen meetings and Zen-Judo/martial-arts sessions in Ramat-Hen, Ramat Gan.

places.name: Community of Mindfulness in Israel (Plum Village)
places.lineage: Plum Village / Vietnamese Thien (Thich Nhat Hanh)
places.notes: Active national Plum Village community with weekly local sanghas in Ein Hod, Hadera, Haifa, Jerusalem (3), Mevasseret, Netanya, Rehovot, and Tel Aviv (2 + youth), plus two online sanghas. Days of mindfulness every ~6 weeks; two annual retreats. Listed on Plum Village's international community pages.

places.name: Hasharon Zen Center (Kwan Um)
places.lineage: Kwan Um School of Zen (Korean Seon)
places.notes: One of the four Kwan Um groups in Israel listed in the Kwan Um School of Zen Wikipedia article and Kwan Um Europe directory. Standalone website not currently resolving; activity coordinated through the Kwan Um Israel network.

places.name: Pardes Hanna Zen Centre (Kwan Um)
places.lineage: Kwan Um School of Zen (Korean Seon)
places.notes: One of four Israeli Kwan Um groups identified in the Kwan Um School of Zen Wikipedia entry; organisationally part of the Kwan Um Europe & Israel region.

places.name: Ramat Gan Zen Group (Kwan Um)
places.lineage: Kwan Um School of Zen (Korean Seon)
places.notes: Smallest of the Israeli Kwan Um groups per Wikipedia; satellite sitting group in the Tel Aviv metro area associated with the Tel Aviv Zen Center.
